nameMeanings.name: Vawdrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Vaudrey
meanings: French, Huguenot: (i) locative name from Vaudrey (Jura). (ii) relationship name possibly in some cases an anglicized spelling of Vaudré(e), from the Germanic personal name Waldrad, composed of waldan ‘ruler’ + rad ‘counsel’.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas Vawdrey was born about 1438, in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom as the son of Mr. Vawdrey and Mrs. Vawdrey. He had at least 5 sons and 4 daughters with Anne Newton.
